title: Has YouTube Really Become the World's Largest Media Company
tldr: Argues YouTube has eclipsed legacy media in scale and influence, reframing how we define a 'media company' in the creator era. Crucial context for anyone thinking about distribution and audience.
why_fresh: reframes media-company definition in creator era
angle: What it means for content strategy when the biggest 'media company' is a UGC platform, not a studio.

title: 87 Percent of Creators Say Creative AI Is Growing Their Business and Audience, According to Adobe's 2026 Creators' Toolkit Report
tldr: Fresh data showing creators overwhelmingly credit AI with audience and revenue growth. A useful, citable stat for the 'AI helps vs. replaces creators' debate.
angle: Unpacking whether creators' optimism about AI holds up against the 'slop' backlash.

title: The Content Framework That Worked In 2019 Is Now Working Against You
tldr: Argues the SEO-era content playbook actively hurts visibility in the AI-search world, demanding a fundamentally new approach. Direct hit on content strategy and decay.
why_fresh: argues old content model now backfires
angle: Auditing your own content pillars for what's quietly become a liability in AI search.

title: Your Measurement Problem isn't Really a Measurement Problem
tldr: Gini Dietrich reframes PR measurement as a systems and enterprise-readiness issue, not a metrics one—and ties it to winning budget. Sharp angle for comms leaders.
angle: Why comms teams lose budget fights even with good dashboards—and how to reframe the conversation.

title: Albertsons Media Collective Unveils Industry-First Branded Entertainment Model, Co-Developed with Procter & Gamble
tldr: A retail media network and P&G build a new branded-entertainment model—a concrete case of brands becoming publishers. Useful campaign breakdown for owned/branded content.
angle: Retail media networks turning into content studios—what brand publishers should learn.

title: The Real Reason Organizations Struggle to Build Brand Trust
tldr: Argues trust comes from emotionally connected, integrated marketing—not content volume. A human-over-polish take relevant to brand narrative and reputation.
angle: Why 'more content' erodes rather than builds trust, and what emotional integration looks like in practice.

title: L'Oréal accelerates generative AI content engine with fresh OpenAI deal
tldr: L'Oréal's CMO is building an AI creative-production engine to stay competitive amid zero-click search. A real-world look at how a giant operationalizes genAI content at scale.
why_fresh: major brand operationalizing genAI at scale
angle: What L'Oréal's AI content engine signals about the future shape of in-house content ops.

title: Why most U.S. workers are checked out and bosses are the last to know
tldr: Explores the persistent gap between how leaders perceive engagement and the disengaged reality—core territory for internal comms and employee engagement.
angle: The perception gap as an internal-comms failure: why leaders don't hear the truth.

title: As AI scrutiny grows, DUDE Wipes points to supply chain savings and productivity gains
tldr: Signals an 'ROI reckoning' for generative AI as brands face harder questions on real business results. Important counter-narrative to AI hype.
angle: The end of AI's honeymoon: how to talk about content-AI ROI honestly.

title: From Content to Conversion: TikTok's New All-in-One Funnel Tools
tldr: TikTok is rebuilding the whole marketing funnel natively, blending content creation, search and direct purchase. A platform shift worth tracking for content strategists.
angle: How TikTok collapsing content-to-conversion changes content planning and measurement.

title: Meghan Gendelman, Canva's B2B CMO, Is Right That Brand Is Now Infrastructure. The Harder Build Comes Next.
tldr: Argues brand has shifted from marketing layer to operational infrastructure, with implications for how companies build and govern it. Provocative framing for brand strategists.
angle: If brand is infrastructure, content governance becomes an engineering discipline.

title: Social Media Statistics 2026: The Numbers Behind 5.79 Billion Users and Where Growth Is Slowing
tldr: Fresh global data showing where social user growth is plateauing—useful baseline for media-consumption and audience-strategy decisions.
angle: What slowing social growth means for content strategy: retention over reach.

title: The creator buzzwords you'll definitely hear at VidCon 2026
tldr: A snapshot of the language and obsessions defining the creator economy this year—useful early-signal read on where creator content is heading.
angle: Decoding creator-economy buzzwords into real shifts content teams should care about.

title: How to Create Content on LinkedIn to Attract Your Ideal Prospects
tldr: A three-part LinkedIn content-funnel framework with specific post formats and posting ratios. Concrete, applicable craft for B2B content.
angle: Building a LinkedIn content funnel that earns reach without chasing vanity engagement.

title: Community invited to help shape Oklahoma City's brand story
tldr: A city crowdsources its brand narrative from residents—a real example of co-created, human-centered storytelling rather than top-down branding.
angle: Co-creating brand narrative with your community: lessons in participatory storytelling.
